$comment: Machine-readable execution spec for relicensing crane (Dokploy fork) to Apache-2.0 by deleting all /proprietary code and neutralizing the license gate (no feature ungating). Line numbers verified against the working tree; deleting lines shifts later ones, so apply top-to-bottom per file or anchor on the 'anchor' string. Decisions: relocate audit-log service (always-on), stub use-whitelabeling, keep enterpriseOnlyResources Set, keep isEnterpriseCloud + Stripe + better-auth sso() plugin + ssoProvider table dormant.

edits.after: Copy the deleted proprietary/audit-log.ts verbatim EXCEPT: (1) delete the import line `import { hasValidLicense } from "@dokploy/server/services/proprietary/license-key";`, and (2) inside createAuditLog delete the two lines `const licensed = await hasValidLicense(input.organizationId);` and `if (!licensed) return;`. Keep db insert into auditLog, getAuditLogs, CreateAuditLogInput, GetAuditLogsInput, and `export type { AuditAction, AuditResourceType };`. Result: audit logging is always-on Apache code.
edits.note: Decision A: relocate + ungate the audit logger to avoid editing ~38 router callers + 2 auth.ts hooks.
Barrel line 38. hasValidLicense no longer needed from the barrel after the gate is removed.
Barrel line 39. No surviving non-proprietary consumer of the sso helpers.
Barrel line 89. The license-server polling cron module is deleted.
Optional: re-export the relocated audit-log service from the barrel. audit.ts/auth.ts import by direct path so this is tidiness, not strictly required.
Line 3.

edits.anchor: resolveRole body after the staticRoles guard (lines ~46-72)
edits.note: SAFER variant: custom-role creation is removed, so any non-static role resolves to null (= old unlicensed behavior). Avoids granting legacy custom-role perms without a license check. Also drop the now-unused `organizationRole` import (line 2) and `and`/`eq` if unused elsewhere — verify with rg before removing.

edits.anchor: enterpriseOnlyResources.has(r) at line ~87 and isPrivilegedRole && enterpriseOnlyResources.has(resource) at line ~194
edits.note: KEEP decision: enterpriseOnlyResources Set stays in access-control.ts; the two bypass usages here are harmless for static roles. Do not alter observable owner/admin/member behavior.
Line 3.

Lines ~384-393. Remove the two enterprise additionalFields from user.additionalFields so generate:drizzle emits a clean schema.
Lines ~514-515. API-key mock-session user object enrichment.
Lines ~564-567. Normal session enrichment.

Line 12. HIDDEN barrel import (no 'proprietary' in the line) that powered enterpriseProcedure.

edits.anchor: * All content that resides under a "/proprietary" directory ...
edits.before: (preamble 'Portions of this software are licensed as follows:' + the /proprietary carve-out bullet on line 5 + Apache section)
edits.after: (full standalone Apache License, Version 2.0 text with the Dokploy copyright line; no /proprietary carve-out, no LICENSE_PROPRIETARY reference)
edits.note: Replace with Apache-2.0 only.

edits.anchor: any commercial resale or redistribution of Dokploy as a service is strictly forbidden
edits.after: (remove the anti-resale restriction so terms match Apache-2.0 permissions; or delete the file)
edits.note: FLAG to owner: rewrite to match Apache-2.0 or delete TERMS_AND_CONDITIONS.md entirely.

fields: KEEP audit_log table + AuditAction/AuditResourceType types (non-proprietary, used by relocated service + audit() helper)
KEEP ssoProvider table / schema/sso.ts (dormant for future Rust SSO rebuild)
schemaMigration.approach: Edit the schema TS files (packages/server/src/db/schema/user.ts + web-server-settings.ts) per the edits[] above, then run `pnpm --filter=dokploy migration:generate` (config apps/dokploy/server/db/drizzle.config.ts) to emit the next forward migration. The generator auto-updates drizzle/meta/_journal.json + writes a new snapshot. DO NOT hand-edit any existing apps/dokploy/drizzle/*.sql or drizzle/meta/*_snapshot.json — they are immutable history (latest existing migration is 0168_long_justice.sql; 66+ historical references to the dropped columns must stay). Expected new file content: ALTER TABLE "user" DROP COLUMN "enableEnterpriseFeatures"; ALTER TABLE "user" DROP COLUMN "licenseKey"; ALTER TABLE "user" DROP COLUMN "isValidEnterpriseLicense"; ALTER TABLE "webServerSettings" DROP COLUMN "whitelabelingConfig";

licenseChanges.action: replace-with-apache-2.0-only (remove /proprietary carve-out clause on line 5 and the 'Portions licensed as follows' preamble; keep full Apache-2.0 text)
remove-anti-resale-clause-or-delete (FLAG to owner: rewrite to match Apache-2.0 permissions, or delete the file)
